qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Node-side PDF generation fetches survey-controlled image URLs and enables SSRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a survey-pdf node-fetch SSRF in image and logo rendering, while the advisory is the inherited jsPDF node loadFile/addImage local-file-read path traversal fixed by upgrading jspdf from ^3.0.1 to ^4.0.0.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The session's only qualifying finding is an SSRF in survey-pdf's Node image-fetch path, not jsPDF's node-side local file inclusion or path traversal behavior.
evidence: GHSA-h3q6-jfrg-3x6q states that survey-pdf is affected because versions <= 1.12.58 and <= 2.5.4 depend on vulnerable jsPDF releases and are fixed in survey-pdf 1.12.59 and 2.5.5.
The vulnerable-to-fixed survey-pdf package.json change is jspdf ^3.0.1 in v1.12.58 and v2.5.4 to jspdf ^4.0.0 in v1.12.59 and v2.5.5.

The underlying jsPDF advisory GHSA-f8cm-6447-x5h2 says attacker control of loadFile and related methods such as addImage, html, and addFont in the Node build enables local file inclusion or path traversal via filesystem reads.

The qualifying finding instead traces survey-controlled imageLink and logo URLs to survey-pdf's src/utils/image/node.ts fetch(url) helper and its PoC demonstrates an outbound HTTP request to http://127.0.0.1:18080/poc.png, which is a different sink and different root cause.
